Speedup: 0.00647%                                                                                        \n          The access patterns for writes to compressible memory are not well suited for the L2 Compression unit. As a   \n          consequence, 4.4x the data written to the L2 cache has to be communicated to the L2 Compression unit. Try     \n          maximizing local coherence for the write operations to compressible memory. For example, avoid writes with    \n          large strides as they lead to partial accesses to many L2 cache lines. Speedup: 0.006295%                                                                                       \n          The access patterns for writes to compressible memory are not well suited for the L2 Compression unit. As a   \n          consequence, 16.9x the data written to the L2 cache has to be communicated to the L2 Compression unit. Try    \n          maximizing local coherence for the write operations to compressible memory. For example, avoid writes with    \n          large strides as they lead to partial accesses to many L2 cache lines. Speedup: 33.33%                                                                                          \n          A wave of thread blocks is defined as the maximum number of blocks that can be executed in parallel on the    \n          target GPU. The number of blocks in a wave depends on the number of multiprocessors and the theoretical       \n          occupancy of the kernel. This kernel launch results in 2 full waves and a partial wave of 1571 thread         \n          blocks. Under the assumption of a uniform execution duration of all thread blocks, this partial wave may      \n          account for up to 33.3% of the total runtime of this kernel. Speedup: 0.0527%                                                                                         \n          The access patterns for writes to compressible memory are not well suited for the L2 Compression unit. Speedup: 0.08165%                                                                                        \n          The access patterns for writes to compressible memory are not well suited for the L2 Compression unit. As a   \n          consequence, 4.1x the data written to the L2 cache has to be communicated to the L2 Compression unit. Try     \n          maximizing local coherence for the write operations to compressible memory.
